_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Rapid irregular heart beat or rapid rate palpitations
Chest pain, especially more marked on exertion

preauth.label: ECG it shows irregular rhythm and abnormal heart rate

claim.label: Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs), Treatment details

cpd_claim.q: Was patient ECG done at the time of/ before discharge?
Did patient's serum electrolytes result reach normal range before discharge?

common_queries: Patient ECG not done at the time of or before discharge
Patient's serum electrolytes did not reach normal range before discharge
Clinical notes missing or incomplete at pre-auth stage
Missing or incomplete blood test results (Coagulation profile/PT, INR or Sr. electrolytes) at pre-auth stage
ECG not showing irregular rhythm or abnormal heart rate, or missing at pre-auth stage
Post-treatment ECG missing at claim stage
X-ray Chest missing at pre-auth stage

clinical_key_pointers: Clinical symptoms: Rapid irregular heart beat
Clinical symptoms: Near syncope or Syncope
Signs: Heart rate >150 b/m or <50 b/m
Risk Factors: Prior valvular heart disease
Risk Factors: Prior Transient Ischemic Attack
additional_information.comorbidity_management: Management of comorbidities like Hypertension (control SBP to less than 140 mmHg), Diabetes Mellitus, COPD, CKD, and Obesity. Also assess for precipitating factors like post (cardiac) surgery, alcoholism/binge drinking, myo-pericarditis/ACS, pneumonitis/pulmonary embolism, sepsis, hyperthyroidism.
additional_information.referral_criteria: Referral from PHC/CHC if indicators for early intervention; Referral from District Hospital if heart rate uncontrolled or presence of CHF or angina.
